verdict: The model has IMPLICIT metacognition (its answer-token distribution tracks competence) but NOT EXPLICIT self-assessment. CONDITION-LEVEL: mean P(answer) tracks accuracy almost perfectly (1.0/0.435/0.288/0.146 vs acc 1.0/0.4/0.193/0.1), while explicit P(True) self-verification is FLAT (~0.4) and even UNDERCONFIDENT on the perfect execute cell (0.422). WITHIN the surface-matched headline cell (familiar_induce, acc 0.4): P(answer) AUROC 0.951 (95% CI (0.9038709308302787, 0.9865643318302931)) vs external surface baseline 0.612 vs explicit P(True) AUROC 0.462. So the model's answer-probability predicts its own per-item correctness beyond surface features (genuine item-level self-knowledge) -- but it CANNOT verbalize/verify it (P(True) is flat). On novel_induce, 0.11 of wrong answers are the NATURAL-successor intrusion (applying the familiar rule instead of the novel one) -- a consistent wrong-rule, not random scatter. DEPLOYABLE: read the model's answer-token probability (implicit) for a usable confidence/abstain signal; do NOT trust its explicit self-assessment.
